raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command that finds files in testbed directory tree, modified >7 days ago, compress in parallel. Use find with -mtime +7, -type f. Then parallel gzip? Use xargs -P? Or GNU parallel. Command: find testbed -type f -mtime +7 -print0 | parallel -0 gzip. Or use -exec? But parallel. So: find testbed -type f -mtime +7 -print0 | parallel -0 gzip. That compresses in place. Output one line.

Then parallel gzip? Use xargs -P? Or GNU parallel. Command: find testbed -type f -mtime +7 -print0 | parallel -0 gzip. Or use -exec? But parallel. So: find testbed -type f -mtime +7 -print0 | parallel -0 gzip. That compresses in place. Output one line.

task.query: Compress in parallel regular files in the testbed directory tree that were last modified more than 7 days ago
task.gold: find /testbed -type f  -mtime +7 | tee compressedP.list | xargs -I{} -P10 compress {} &
